reasoning: While Disney and the NFL reached a major non-binding agreement in August 2025 that was approved by NFL owners on October 22, 2025, taking 'control' of the NFL Media unit before the end of 2025 is practically impossible. The transaction requires a substantive antitrust review by the U.S. Department of Justice. Disney's Head of Corporate Development publicly stated that late 2026 is their 'best case' for closing the deal due to regulatory hurdles. Under U.S. antitrust law (the HSR Act), the parties cannot legally transfer operational control (known as 'gun-jumping') prior to regulatory clearance. Furthermore, only parts of the NFL's media suite (e.g., NFL Network, RedZone) are being acquired, while the NFL is retaining others (NFL Films, NFL+, etc.), raising doubts about whether the 'NFL Media unit' as a whole transfers. The Manifold market trading at 87% in late October was highly likely a naive overreaction to the 'owners approve deal' headline, ignoring the strict timeline for closing and regulatory clearance requirements.
